peptides.name: ACE 031
peptides.research: ACE-031 is a decoy receptor for myostatin and related proteins. Clinical trials showed rapid muscle mass increases (3+ lbs lean mass in 1 month) in muscular dystrophy patients. Acts by binding myostatin before it can inhibit muscle growth.
peptides.mechanism: ACE-031 is a soluble form of the Activin receptor type IIB (ActRIIB) that acts as a decoy receptor. It binds circulating myostatin, activins, and other TGF-beta superfamily members that limit muscle growth, preventing them from signaling to muscle cells.

peptides.name: Adamax
peptides.research: Adamax is a synthetic analog of Semax with enhanced potency and duration. Developed from ACTH (4-10) fragment with added adamantane group for improved BBB penetration. Russian research shows cognitive enhancement, neuroprotection, and anxiolytic effects.
peptides.mechanism: Adamax is a modified ACTH fragment with adamantane moiety for enhanced lipophilicity and BBB penetration. It modulates BDNF expression, improves cerebral circulation, and has neuroprotective properties. The adamantane group extends duration of action.

peptides.name: Adipotide
peptides.research: Adipotide is a peptidomimetic designed to induce apoptosis in blood vessels supplying white adipose tissue (fat). Preclinical studies in monkeys showed rapid fat loss by cutting off blood supply to fat cells. Experimental/research only.
peptides.mechanism: Adipotide binds to prohibitin and annexin A2 receptors on blood vessel endothelial cells in white adipose tissue, triggering apoptosis of the blood vessels. This cuts off blood supply to fat cells, causing them to die and be reabsorbed.
pros: Rapid fat loss in animal studies
Does not affect muscle or other tissue
cons: May affect other tissues with similar receptors
Rapid fat loss may cause gallstones

peptides.name: AICAR (Acadesine)
peptides.research: AICAR is an AMPK (AMP-activated protein kinase) activator studied for metabolic benefits, exercise mimetics, and cardioprotection. Research shows enhanced fat oxidation, improved insulin sensitivity, and endurance enhancement in animal studies.
peptides.mechanism: AICAR is taken up by cells and converted to ZMP, which mimics AMP and activates AMPK - the cellular energy sensor. AMPK activation promotes fat oxidation, glucose uptake, mitochondrial biogenesis, and autophagy. Called "exercise in a pill" in research.
inst: Morning dosing may enhance fat oxidation
Combine with exercise for synergistic effects

peptides.name: AOD-9604
peptides.research: AOD-9604 is the C-terminus fragment of HGH (amino acids 177-191). Clinical trials show fat oxidation without affecting blood glucose or IGF-1. Originally developed as anti-obesity drug with favorable safety profile.
peptides.mechanism: AOD-9604 mimics the fat-burning region of natural GH without the growth-promoting or blood sugar effects. It stimulates lipolysis (fat breakdown) and inhibits lipogenesis (fat storage) through modulation of beta-3-adrenergic receptors and other pathways.
pros: Burns fat without affecting blood glucose
Does NOT raise IGF-1 (safer than GH)
No impact on natural GH production
FDA granted Generally Recognized As Safe (GRAS) status
cons: Effects more modest than GLP-1 drugs
Must be combined with diet for results
Less potent than modern weight loss peptides
warnings: Generally recognized as safest weight loss peptide
inst: Works best with caloric deficit and exercise

peptides.name: ARA290 (Cibinetide)
peptides.research: ARA290 is a synthetic peptide that mimics the tissue-protective effects of erythropoietin (EPO) without stimulating red blood cell production. Clinical trials for neuropathic pain (small fiber neuropathy) and tissue protection. FDA Breakthrough Therapy designation.
peptides.mechanism: ARA290 binds to the tissue-protective heteromeric receptor (EPOR/CD131) without activating the classical EPOR homodimer responsible for erythropoiesis. This provides tissue protection, nerve regeneration, and pain relief without increasing hematocrit or blood viscosity.
pros: Tissue protective effects without RBC increase
cons: Effects take weeks for pain relief
inst: Commit to 12 weeks for full effects
Can combine with other nerve support
Monitor pain levels and nerve function

peptides.name: B7-33
peptides.research: B7-33 is a synthetic peptide derived from the HSP20 protein family. Research shows potent anti-fibrotic effects, particularly in cardiac and pulmonary fibrosis models. It inhibits TGF-beta signaling pathway to prevent fibrotic tissue deposition.
peptides.mechanism: B7-33 acts as a competitive inhibitor of TGF-beta signaling by binding to the TGF-beta receptor complex. This prevents downstream fibrotic signaling, reducing collagen deposition and fibroblast activation in tissues prone to fibrosis.
inst: Combine with other anti-fibrotics if indicated

peptides.name: CJC1295 NO DAC + Ipamorelin Blend
peptides.research: Pre-mixed blend: CJC-1295 NO DAC 5mg + Ipamorelin 5mg per vial. Combines pulsatile GH release of Mod GRF with selective GHRP action of Ipamorelin.
peptides.mechanism: Dual mechanism: CJC NO DAC creates pulsatile GH elevation via GHRH stimulation. Ipamorelin adds selective ghrelin receptor activation without cortisol/prolactin elevation. Together they optimize GH secretion patterns.
inst: Contains 5mg CJC-1295 NO DAC + 5mg Ipamorelin (10mg total)
Typical dose: 300mcg (0.3mg) of each peptide per injection
Inject 2-3 times daily on empty stomach
Empty stomach: wait 30 min after injection before eating

peptides.name: BPC 157 + TB 500 Blend
peptides.research: Pre-mixed blend: BPC-157 5mg + TB-500 5mg per vial. Combines local tissue repair with systemic stem cell activation.
peptides.mechanism: BPC-157 promotes local angiogenesis and collagen synthesis at injury sites, while TB-500 mobilizes stem cells and enhances systemic tissue regeneration through actin regulation.
warnings: Same cancer precautions as individual peptides
inst: Typical dose: 1mg total (500mcg of each peptide) per injection
Can dose higher initially for acute injuries

peptides.name: BPC 157 + TB 500 Blend (High Dose)
peptides.research: Pre-mixed blend: BPC-157 10mg + TB-500 10mg per vial. Higher dose version for severe injuries or faster recovery protocols.
peptides.mechanism: Same synergistic mechanism: BPC-157 provides local tissue repair while TB-500 mobilizes stem cells systemically. Higher concentration allows lower volume per injection.
warnings: Same cancer precautions as individual peptides
inst: Typical dose: 1.5mg total (750mcg of each peptide) per injection
Lower volume per injection vs standard blend
Ideal for severe injuries or faster recovery

peptides.name: BPC 157
peptides.research: Over 120 peer-reviewed studies demonstrate accelerated healing via the nitric oxide pathway. Originally derived from human gastric juice, BPC-157 shows remarkable tissue repair properties across tendon, ligament, muscle, and intestinal tissue. Studies report 30-40% faster wound healing in animal models.
peptides.mechanism: BPC-157 activates the VEGF (vascular endothelial growth factor) pathway, enhancing nitric oxide synthesis and angiogenesis. It upregulates growth hormone receptors and promotes tendon cell proliferation. The peptide modulates dopaminergic and serotonergic systems, contributing to its systemic healing effects.
pros: Accelerates tendon and ligament healing by 30-40%
No known significant side effects at therapeutic doses
Systemic and local effects depending on injection site
cons: Must be administered on empty stomach
Most effective when injected near injury site
Banned by WADA for competitive athletes
warnings: Pregnancy and breastfeeding: insufficient safety data
Blood clotting disorders: may affect fibrinolysis
inst: Inject subcutaneously near injury site OR abdominal fat
Can be stacked with TB-500 for enhanced healing

peptides.name: Cagrilintide
peptides.research: Cagrilintide is a long-acting amylin analog developed by Novo Nordisk. Phase 3 trials show significant weight loss when combined with semaglutide (CagriSema). Amylin acts on brain satiety centers and slows gastric emptying.
peptides.mechanism: Cagrilintide mimics human amylin, a hormone co-secreted with insulin from pancreatic beta cells. It acts on amylin receptors in the brainstem to induce satiety, reduces glucagon secretion, and slows gastric emptying. The long-acting formulation allows weekly dosing.
inst: Start low and titrate every 2 weeks
Combine with semaglutide for maximum effect
Take on same day each week
Monitor for nausea - may need antiemetics initially

peptides.name: Cagrisema (Cagrilintide + Semaglutide)
peptides.research: Pre-mixed blend: Cagrilintide 2.5mg + Semaglutide 2.5mg per vial. Combines amylin analog (Cagrilintide) appetite suppression with GLP-1 (Semaglutide) effects. CagriSema Phase 3 trials show superior weight loss to either drug alone.
peptides.mechanism: Dual mechanism: Cagrilintide mimics amylin for satiety and slowed gastric emptying. Semaglutide activates GLP-1 receptors for appetite suppression and enhanced insulin secretion. Together they provide complementary pathways for weight management.
inst: Typical starting dose: 0.25mg total per week
Start low and titrate every 2-4 weeks
Take on same day each week
Monitor for nausea - may need antiemetics initially

peptides.name: CJC1295 DAC
peptides.research: Clinical studies demonstrate sustained GH elevation for up to 6 days after single injection. The DAC (Drug Affinity Complex) extends half-life from minutes to days by binding to albumin. Research shows 2-10x increase in GH levels with minimal side effects.
peptides.mechanism: CJC-1295 is a GHRH (growth hormone releasing hormone) analog with DAC modification that significantly extends its half-life. It stimulates the pituitary to release GH in a more sustained manner compared to natural GHRH. The DAC component binds to albumin, creating a depot effect.
pros: Elevates baseline GH for sustained effect
Long half-life due to DAC modification
cons: More expensive than regular CJC-1295 (no DAC)
warnings: Water retention: caution with heart/kidney issues
Carpal tunnel syndrome: may worsen symptoms
inst: Can cause initial water retention (usually temporary)
Best stacked with GHRP for enhanced GH release

peptides.name: CJC-1295 NO DAC (Mod GRF 1-29)
peptides.research: CJC-1295 without DAC (also called Modified GRF 1-29) is the shorter-acting version. Clinical research shows it creates a natural GH pulse without the prolonged elevation of the DAC version. Preferred by some for more natural GH patterns and less water retention.
peptides.mechanism: Mod GRF 1-29 is a modified GHRH (1-29) with enhanced stability. It stimulates pituitary GH release in a pulsatile fashion similar to natural GHRH. The lack of DAC means shorter duration but more natural GH patterns without prolonged elevation.
pros: Less water retention than DAC version
Stack with GHRP for synergistic effect
cons: Must be combined with GHRP for best results
Effects more subtle than DAC version
warnings: Water retention: less than DAC but possible
General: safer profile than DAC version
inst: Creates pulsatile GH release - natural pattern

peptides.name: Cortagen
peptides.research: Cortagen is a synthetic tripeptide bioregulator for the adrenal cortex. Russian research shows normalization of adrenal function, improved stress response, and regulation of cortisol production in patients with adrenal dysfunction.
peptides.mechanism: Cortagen acts on adrenal cortex cells to normalize hormone synthesis. It helps regulate the synthesis of glucocorticoids and mineralocorticoids, supporting healthy stress response and electrolyte balance without suppressing natural function.
warnings: General: not replacement for adrenal meds

peptides.name: Crystagen
peptides.research: Crystagen is a synthetic tripeptide bioregulator for the immune system. Russian clinical studies show improved immune response, enhanced T-cell function, and normalization of immune activity in patients with immune dysregulation.
peptides.mechanism: Crystagen acts on immune cells to normalize proliferation and differentiation. It supports thymus function and T-cell maturation, helping regulate both underactive and overactive immune responses for balanced immune surveillance.
warnings: General: not replacement for immune therapy

peptides.name: Dermorphin
peptides.research: Dermorphin is a heptapeptide found in South American tree frog skin with potent mu-opioid activity (40x morphine). Research only - not approved for human use. Extremely potent opioid effects with short duration.
peptides.mechanism: Dermorphin is a potent and selective mu-opioid receptor (MOR) agonist with approximately 40x the potency of morphine. It produces analgesia through the same pathways as traditional opioids but with different selectivity and side effect profiles.
cons: Research only - not for human use

peptides.name: Dihexa
peptides.research: Dihexa is a synthetic hepatocyte growth factor (HGF) mimetic developed at Washington State University. Research shows 7x more potent than BDNF for synaptogenesis. Studies demonstrate enhanced memory formation, cognitive performance, and potential neuroprotective effects.
peptides.mechanism: Dihexa crosses the blood-brain barrier and activates the HGF/c-MET pathway, promoting synaptogenesis (formation of new synapses) and neurogenesis. It enhances dendritic arborization and has shown 7x the synapse-forming activity of brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF).
pros: 7x more potent than BDNF for synaptogenesis
cons: Effects may take weeks to notice
warnings: Cancer: HGF pathway may theoretically support

peptides.name: DSIP
peptides.research: Delta Sleep-Inducing Peptide discovered in 1963. Research shows promotion of slow-wave (deep) sleep without sedation or hangover. May modulate stress hormones and improve recovery. Studied for insomnia, withdrawal, and stress-related conditions.
peptides.mechanism: DSIP crosses the blood-brain barrier and modulates sleep-wake cycles by interacting with GABAergic and monoaminergic systems. It increases slow-wave sleep (stages 3-4) without altering REM sleep. Also influences ACTH/cortisol and may have anti-stress effects.
warnings: General caution due to CNS effects
inst: Can combine with other sleep hygiene practices

peptides.name: Dulaglutide (Trulicity)
peptides.research: FDA approved 2014 for type 2 diabetes. Weekly GLP-1 agonist with proven cardiovascular benefits (REDUCES major CV events). REWIND trial showed reduced stroke/MI in diabetics with CV risk. Now studied for weight loss and prediabetes.
peptides.mechanism: Dulaglutide is a long-acting GLP-1 receptor agonist with albumin-binding domain for extended half-life. It enhances glucose-dependent insulin secretion, suppresses glucagon, slows gastric emptying, and promotes satiety. Cardiovascular benefits beyond glucose lowering.
cons: Less weight loss than semaglutide (~10% vs 15%)
inst: Take with food if nausea occurs

peptides.name: EPO (Erythropoietin)
peptides.research: Recombinant human erythropoietin is FDA approved for anemia in kidney disease, cancer, and other conditions. Stimulates red blood cell production in bone marrow. Well-established clinical use with defined protocols.
peptides.mechanism: EPO binds to the erythropoietin receptor (EPOR) on erythroid progenitor cells in bone marrow, promoting survival, proliferation, and differentiation of red blood cell precursors. Increases hematocrit and oxygen-carrying capacity.

peptides.name: Follistatin 344
peptides.research: Follistatin inhibits myostatin, the protein that limits muscle growth. Research in muscular dystrophy and muscle wasting shows dramatic increases in muscle mass when myostatin is blocked. Some subjects gained 20%+ lean mass in studies.
peptides.mechanism: Follistatin binds to and neutralizes myostatin (GDF-8) and activin A, removing the "brakes" on muscle growth. By inhibiting these negative regulators, it allows muscles to grow beyond normal genetic limits. Also affects follicle-stimulating hormone.
pros: Potential for muscle mass beyond genetic limits
Research shows dramatic gains in muscle wasting
May improve muscle quality in aging
warnings: Tendons: rapid muscle growth may outpace tendon strength
Cancer: removing growth limits theoretically risky
inst: Often stacked with IGF-1 for synergistic growth
Monitor for tendon issues as muscles grow faster

peptides.name: FOXO4-DRI (Senolytic)
peptides.research: FOXO4-DRI is a senolytic peptide that induces apoptosis in senescent ("zombie") cells. Research published in Cell (2017) showed extended lifespan and restored physical capacity in aging mice. Selectively targets harmful senescent cells while sparing healthy cells.
peptides.mechanism: FOXO4-DRI interferes with the FOXO4-p53 interaction that keeps senescent cells alive. By blocking this interaction, p53 is freed to trigger apoptosis (programmed cell death) specifically in senescent cells. This clears aged cells without affecting healthy tissue.
warnings: Immune suppression: clears immune senescent cells
inst: Higher doses less frequently may be optimal

peptides.name: Fragment 17-23
peptides.research: Fragment 17-23 is a modified version of the HGH Fragment 176-191 with additional amino acids. Research suggests enhanced fat oxidation and metabolic effects compared to the shorter fragment. Limited clinical data available.
peptides.mechanism: Fragment 17-23 contains the full fat-burning region of HGH (amino acids 177-191) plus additional stabilizing residues. It mimics the lipolytic effects of natural GH without affecting blood glucose or IGF-1 levels, potentially with enhanced stability.

peptides.name: GHRP-2
peptides.research: Considered the most potent of the GHRP family with high affinity for the ghrelin receptor. Studies show 3-5x GH release compared to baseline. However, it raises cortisol and prolactin, requiring careful consideration for users sensitive to these hormones.
peptides.mechanism: GHRP-2 is a potent synthetic agonist of the ghrelin receptor (GHS-R1a). It triggers massive pulsatile GH release by mimicking ghrelin, the "hunger hormone." Unlike Ipamorelin, it also stimulates ACTH and prolactin release, creating a more pronounced but less selective GH response.
cons: Strong hunger stimulation may be unwanted
warnings: High cortisol: caution with adrenal issues
inst: Administer 2-3 times daily on empty stomach
Prepare for significant hunger after dosing
Consider P5P supplement to manage prolactin

peptides.name: GHRP-6
peptides.research: Extensively studied for GH deficiency and cachexia. Known primarily for intense appetite stimulation via ghrelin mimicry. Clinical use in patients needing weight gain. GH-releasing potency similar to GHRP-2 but with more pronounced hunger effects.
peptides.mechanism: GHRP-6 binds to ghrelin receptors in the hypothalamus and pituitary, triggering GH release AND intense hunger signaling. The appetite stimulation is a direct result of ghrelin receptor activation in feeding centers. Creates significant GH pulses but with cortisol/prolactin elevation.
pros: Strong GH release comparable to GHRP-2
warnings: Eating disorders: may trigger unhealthy patterns
inst: Inject when you want to eat (timing critical)
Have food ready - hunger hits within 30 minutes
Pre-workout can provide energy but may cause hypoglycemia
Consider if struggling to consume enough calories

peptides.name: Glutathione (Reduced)
peptides.research: Glutathione is the body\
peptides.mechanism: Glutathione is a tripeptide (glutamate-cysteine-glycine) that neutralizes free radicals, supports phase II liver detoxification, and regenerates other antioxidants (vitamin C, E). It binds to toxins for elimination and maintains cellular redox balance.
cons: Very short half-life when oral (poor absorption)
inst: Subcutaneous or IM injection (IV preferred for high doses)
Combine with vitamin C for synergy

peptides.name: Gonadorelin
peptides.research: Gonadorelin is synthetic GnRH that stimulates the pituitary to release LH and FSH. Used for fertility treatments, hypogonadism testing, and PCT. Short-acting version requires pulsatile administration unlike long-acting analogs.
peptides.mechanism: Gonadorelin binds to GnRH receptors in the anterior pituitary, stimulating pulsatile release of LH and FSH. This stimulates gonadal function and testosterone production. Short half-life requires frequent administration to maintain natural pulsatility.

peptides.name: HCG (Human Chorionic Gonadotropin)
peptides.research: HCG is FDA approved for fertility treatments and hypogonadism. Structurally similar to LH, it stimulates Leydig cells to produce testosterone and maintains testicular function. Widely used in TRT and PCT protocols.
peptides.mechanism: HCG binds to LH receptors on Leydig cells in the testes, stimulating testosterone production and maintaining testicular size and function. Unlike exogenous testosterone, it preserves fertility and natural hormone axis while supporting testosterone levels.

peptides.name: Hexarelin
peptides.research: Research shows hexarelin is the most potent GHRP, even stronger than GHRP-2. Unique among GHRPs for cardioprotective effects via activation of CD36 receptors in cardiac tissue. Studies demonstrate protection against ischemia-reperfusion injury.
peptides.mechanism: Hexarelin binds to GHS-R1a with highest affinity of all GHRPs, producing massive GH release. Additionally, it activates CD36 receptors in the heart, providing cardioprotective effects independent of GH. This dual mechanism makes it unique in the GHRP family.
cons: Not suitable for long-term continuous use
Limited availability compared to other GHRPs
warnings: Rapid receptor desensitization - strict cycling required
Heart conditions: beneficial but consult cardiologist
inst: Most potent - start with lower dose
Best stacked with GHRH for amplified GH release

peptides.name: HGH (Human Growth Hormone)
peptides.research: Synthetic human growth hormone (somatropin) is FDA approved for GH deficiency, muscle wasting, and short bowel syndrome. Decades of clinical use with established protocols. Direct GH administration vs stimulating natural production.
peptides.mechanism: Exogenous HGH directly elevates circulating growth hormone and IGF-1 levels without relying on pituitary function. It binds to GH receptors throughout the body, promoting anabolism, lipolysis, and anti-aging effects. Bypasses natural regulatory mechanisms.
warnings: Carpal tunnel: common at higher doses

peptides.name: HMG (Human Menopausal Gonadotropin)
peptides.research: HMG contains both FSH and LH, making it superior to HCG alone for fertility and testosterone production. Used in fertility treatments and post-cycle therapy (PCT) to restore natural testosterone production.
peptides.mechanism: HMG contains follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH) and luteinizing hormone (LH). FSH stimulates Sertoli cells and sperm production, while LH stimulates Leydig cells to produce testosterone. More comprehensive than HCG which only mimics LH.
cons: May cause testicular enlargement (desired but uncomfortable)
inst: Use during PCT or fertility treatment

peptides.name: Melanotan II
peptides.research: Synthetic analog of alpha-MSH (melanocyte stimulating hormone). Clinical trials for melanoma prevention (increased pigmentation = protection) and sexual dysfunction. FDA has not approved it, but research shows consistent tanning and libido enhancement.
peptides.mechanism: Melanotan II binds to melanocortin receptors (MC1R for tanning, MC3R/MC4R for sexual function). Activation of MC1R stimulates melanin production in skin. MC3R/MC4R activation in the brain enhances sexual arousal in both men and women through CNS pathways.
pros: Produces natural-looking tan without sun exposure
May reduce skin cancer risk (increased melanin)
Enhances libido in men and women
Only need maintenance doses after loading
cons: Spontaneous erections in men (can be unwanted)
warnings: Moles: must monitor for changes (melanoma risk)
Erections lasting >4 hours: seek medical help
inst: Loading phase: 500mcg daily until desired tan (2-3 weeks)
Start LOW to assess nausea (250mcg first dose)
Sun exposure accelerates tanning once started

peptides.name: MOTS-C
peptides.research: MOTS-C is a mitochondrial-derived peptide encoded by mitochondrial DNA. Discovered in 2015, research shows it regulates metabolism, improves insulin sensitivity, and may protect against metabolic syndrome and age-related decline.
peptides.mechanism: MOTS-C is imported from mitochondria to nucleus where it regulates gene expression for metabolic homeostasis. It activates AMPK (cellular energy sensor), improving glucose uptake and fatty acid oxidation. Acts as a mitochondrial signaling molecule.
cons: Effects subtle compared to other peptides
warnings: General caution due to limited data
inst: Morning dosing may enhance energy metabolism
Combine with exercise for synergistic AMPK activation
Consider with other mitochondrial support supplements

peptides.name: NAD+ (Nicotinamide Adenine Dinucleotide)
peptides.research: NAD+ is an essential coenzyme found in every living cell. Declines 50% by age 50. Essential for mitochondrial function, DNA repair via PARP enzymes, and sirtuin activation (longevity genes). IV NAD+ has shown benefits in addiction recovery, chronic fatigue, and anti-aging.
peptides.mechanism: NAD+ is the oxidized form of nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide. Required for cellular respiration (mitochondrial ATP production), DNA repair enzyme activation (PARPs), and sirtuin deacetylase activity. Higher NAD+ levels support metabolic health, genomic stability, and cellular stress resistance.
pros: Essential coenzyme for cellular energy (ATP production)
Declines with age - supplementation restores levels
cons: Effects may take weeks to notice
Oral NR/NMN may be more practical for some
warnings: Cancer: high NAD+ may theoretically support cancer metabolism
Methylation: high doses may deplete methyl groups
inst: Subcutaneous injection (IV more common but invasive)
Large injection volume - split doses if needed
Combine with TMG or methyl donors

peptides.name: PEG MGF
peptides.research: Mechano Growth Factor (MGF) is the splice variant of IGF-1 produced in response to mechanical stress (training). PEGylation extends its half-life from minutes to days. Research shows enhanced satellite cell activation and muscle repair after damage.
peptides.mechanism: PEG-MGF activates satellite cells and promotes muscle stem cell proliferation. The PEG (polyethylene glycol) modification prevents degradation and extends circulation time. Works primarily through local autocrine/paracrine mechanisms at muscle tissue level.
pros: Activates muscle satellite cells for repair/growth
Lower systemic effects than IGF-1 LR3
cons: Intramuscular injection into trained muscle required
inst: Inject INTO the muscle trained that day
Typically 2-3 times per week per muscle group
Can combine with systemic IGF-1 or GH peptides

peptides.name: PT-141 (Bremelanotide)
peptides.research: FDA approved 2019 for premenopausal women with hypoactive sexual desire disorder (HSDD). Works on melanocortin receptors in CNS unlike Viagra (vascular). Clinical trials show significant improvement in sexual desire and satisfaction.
peptides.mechanism: PT-141 is a synthetic melanocortin analog that activates MC3R and MC4R receptors in the central nervous system. Unlike PDE5 inhibitors (Viagra), it works on desire pathways in the brain rather than blood flow, addressing psychological/libido aspects of sexual dysfunction.
pros: FDA approved for HSDD in women (2019)
Works via CNS (brain) not vascular system
Works in both men and women
cons: FDA approval limited to women (off-label for men)
inst: Take with anti-nausea medication if needed

peptides.name: Retatrutide
peptides.research: Phase 3 trials show up to 24% weight loss - highest of any obesity medication. Triple agonist targeting GLP-1, GIP, and glucagon receptors. Creates thermogenic effect alongside appetite suppression.
peptides.mechanism: Retatrutide activates three receptors: GLP-1 (satiety/glucose), GIP (insulin secretion/enhancement), and glucagon (energy expenditure/lipolysis). The glucagon component adds thermogenic fat burning not seen in dual agonists, creating unprecedented weight loss.
pros: Most potent weight loss peptide (24% in trials)
Superior to both Semaglutide and Tirzepatide
Preserves lean muscle better than predecessors
cons: Very new - limited long-term safety data
warnings: Heart rate: sustained elevation requires monitoring
Thyroid cancer: same GLP-1 class precautions
inst: Monitor resting heart rate - elevation expected

peptides.name: Sermorelin
peptides.research: FDA-approved since 1997 with extensive safety data. Clinical trials show restoration of youthful GH levels in adults with GH deficiency. Unlike exogenous GH, sermorelin preserves the natural pulsatile pattern and negative feedback loops, making it safer long-term.
peptides.mechanism: Sermorelin is a synthetic analog of GHRH (1-29 amino acids) that stimulates the pituitary to produce and release endogenous GH. It preserves the body\
pros: Can be used continuously without losing effectiveness
cons: Must be administered at bedtime for optimal results
Takes 3-6 months to see full effects
Less dramatic results than synthetic GH
warnings: None known - considered safest GH option
inst: BEDTIME INJECTION ONLY (mimics natural GH pulse)
Rotate injection sites to prevent lipodystrophy
Can be combined with Ipamorelin for enhanced effect

peptides.name: TB 500
peptides.research: Thymosin Beta-4 has been extensively studied in clinical trials for wound healing and cardiac repair. FDA has granted orphan drug status for TB-4 in epidermolysis bullosa. Research shows it accelerates keratinocyte migration and promotes actin polymerization in healing tissues.
peptides.mechanism: TB-500 binds to actin monomers, promoting cell migration and tissue regeneration. It regulates G-actin/F-actin ratios, enabling cellular mobility critical for wound closure. Additionally, it upregulates vascular endothelial growth factor and promotes stem cell differentiation.
pros: Promotes stem cell migration and differentiation
Improves flexibility and reduces tissue fibrosis
Shown cardioprotective effects in animal studies
Effective for chronic injuries unresponsive to other treatments
cons: Requires loading phase for optimal results
warnings: Heart conditions: monitor if cardiac issues present
inst: Loading phase: 7.5mg (1.5 vials) split over first week
Best stacked with BPC-157 for acute injuries
Can continue longer cycles with 4-week breaks

peptides.name: Tesamorelin
peptides.research: FDA approved (2010) specifically for HIV-associated lipodystrophy. Clinical trials showed significant reduction in visceral adipose tissue (VAT) by 18% over 26 weeks. Also improves triglycerides and cholesterol markers. Only peptide specifically indicated for abdominal fat reduction.
peptides.mechanism: Tesamorelin is a synthetic GHRH analog that stimulates endogenous GH production. It specifically targets visceral fat through enhanced lipolysis and improved metabolic function. The peptide also improves lipid profiles and may enhance insulin sensitivity.
pros: FDA approved for visceral fat reduction
18% reduction in visceral adipose tissue in trials
Also increases IGF-1 for anabolic benefits
cons: Joint pain reported in some users
inst: Subcutaneous injection in abdominal area (target zone)
Bedtime preferred but morning also acceptable
Combine with diet/exercise for optimal VAT reduction
Regular monitoring of IGF-1 and glucose recommended

peptides.name: Thymalin
peptides.research: Thymalin is a synthetic version of thymic peptide originally isolated from calf thymus. Russian research over decades shows immune system modulation and potential lifespan extension. Particularly studied in elderly for immune restoration.
peptides.mechanism: Thymalin regulates T-cell differentiation and maturation by mimicking natural thymic hormones. It restores thymic function which declines with age (thymic involution), supporting naive T-cell production and immune surveillance against pathogens and abnormal cells.
pros: May enhance vaccine responses in elderly
cons: Effects on healthy immune system unclear
Subtle effects (not dramatic like GH peptides)
Often combined with Epitalon for synergy
inst: Often stacked with Epitalon for longevity synergy
Best in morning on empty stomach

peptides.name: Tirzepatide
peptides.research: SURPASS trials show 20%+ weight loss, superior to Semaglutide. Dual mechanism targeting both GIP and GLP-1 receptors. FDA approved 2022 for diabetes, 2023 for obesity. Faster and greater weight loss than single-hormone approaches.
peptides.mechanism: Tirzepatide is the first dual GIP/GLP-1 receptor agonist. GIP (glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide) enhances GLP-1 effects. Combined action provides superior glucose control, appetite suppression, and fat oxidation compared to GLP-1 alone.
pros: More potent than Semaglutide (20%+ weight loss)
Better glycemic control than GLP-1 alone
cons: More GI side effects than Semaglutide
warnings: Thyroid cancer: same precautions as other GLP-1s
inst: Monitor for GI tolerance at each escalation
